- Green (N) is connected to the player.
- Blue (b) is connected to Green.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Board blocks:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) < 50 and node['color'] in ['N', 'b', 'O', 'R', 'M']:
        if node['color'] == 'M' and node['pixels'] == 18:
            print(f"  Player: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
        elif node['color'] == 'M' and node['pixels'] == 4:
            pass
        else:
            print(f"  {node['color']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check adjacency
print("\nAdjacency (through c):")
for a, b in seg['adjacency_list']:
    na = seg['nodes'][a]
    nb = seg['nodes'][b]
    if na['color'] == 'c' or nb['color'] == 'c':
        if na['pixels'] >= 4 and nb['pixels'] >= 4:
            print(f"  {na['color']} <-> {nb['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
